sections.section: 2. SCOPE AND USE OF THIS CODE
sections.summary: Heading only: scope and use title with no substantive text provided.
Heading only: definitions section title with no substantive definitions in this entry.
Defines 'Abattoir' as any establishment where specified animals are slaughtered and dressed for human consumption and that is approved, registered and/or listed by the competent authority.
Definition heading stating 'Animals of the following types:' but the specific types are not provided in the text excerpt.
Defines 'Carcass' as the body of an animal after dressing.
Defines 'Chemical residues' as residues of veterinary drugs and pesticides, referencing the Codex Alimentarius definitions.
Defines 'Competent authority' as the official government authority charged with control of meat hygiene, including setting and enforcing regulatory meat hygiene requirements.
Defines 'Competent body' as a body officially recognised and overseen by the competent authority to undertake specified meat hygiene activities.
Defines 'Competent person' as a person who has the training, knowledge, skills and ability to perform an assigned task and who is subject to requirements specified by the competent authority.

Defines 'Condemned' as inspected and judged by a competent person, or otherwise determined by the competent authority, to be unsafe or unsuitable for human consumption and requiring appropriate disposal.
Defines 'Contaminant' as any biological or chemical agent, foreign matter, or other substance not intentionally added to food that may compromise food safety or suitability.
Defines 'Disease or defect' as any abnormality affecting safety and/or suitability.
Defines 'Dressing' as the progressive separation of the body of an animal into a carcass and other edible and inedible parts.

Defines 'Equivalence' as the capability of different meat hygiene systems to meet the same food safety and/or suitability objectives and also includes a definition of 'Establishment' as a building or area used for performing meat hygiene activities that is approved, registered and/or listed by the competent authority.
Defines 'Establishment' as a building or area used for performing meat hygiene activities that is approved, registered and/or listed by the competent authority for such purposes.
Defines 'Establishment operator' as the person in control of an establishment who is responsible for ensuring that the regulatory meat hygiene requirements are met.

Defines 'Food safety objective (FSO)' as the maximum frequency and/or concentration of a hazard in a food at the time of consumption that provides or contributes to the appropriate level of protection (ALOP).
Defines 'Fresh Meat' as meat that, apart from refrigeration, has not been treated for preservation other than through protective packaging and which retains its natural characteristics.

Defines “Game depot” as a building in which killed wild game is temporarily held prior to transfer to an establishment and which is approved, registered and/or listed by the competent authority, noting it is a particular type of establishment under the Code.
Defines Good Hygienic Practice (GHP) as all practices regarding the conditions and measures necessary to ensure the safety and suitability of food at all stages of the food chain.

Defines “Hazard” as a biological, chemical or physical agent or condition of food with the potential to cause an adverse health effect, and also includes a definition of “Hunter” as a person involved in killing, bleeding, partial evisceration and partial field dressing of killed wild game.
Defines “Hunter” as a person involved in the killing and/or bleeding, partial evisceration and partial field dressing of killed wild game.
Defines “Inedible” as material inspected and judged by a competent person, or otherwise determined by the competent authority, to be unsuitable for human consumption.
Defines “Manufactured Meat” as products resulting from processing raw meat or further processing such that the cut surface no longer shows the characteristics of fresh meat.
Defines “Meat” as all parts of an animal that are intended for, or have been judged as, safe and suitable for human consumption.
Defines “Meat hygiene” as all conditions and measures necessary to ensure the safety and suitability of meat at all stages of the food chain.
Defines “Meat preparation” as raw meat to which foodstuffs, seasonings or additives have been added.

Defines “Mechanically separated meat (MSM)” as product obtained by mechanically removing meat from flesh-bearing bones after boning or from poultry carcasses in a way that causes loss or modification of muscle fibre structure.
Defines “Minced meat” as boneless meat that has been reduced into fragments.

Defines “Official inspector” as a competent person appointed, accredited or otherwise recognized by the competent authority to perform official meat hygiene activities on behalf of, or under the supervision of, the competent authority.
Defines “Organoleptic inspection” as the use of sight, touch, taste and smell to identify diseases and defects.

Defines “Performance criterion” as the required effect on the frequency and/or concentration of a hazard achieved by applying one or more control measures to provide or contribute to a performance objective or food safety objective.

Defines “Performance objective” as the maximum frequency and/or concentration of a hazard in a food at a specified step before consumption that provides or contributes to a food safety objective or appropriate level of protection.

Defines “Post-mortem inspection” as any procedure or test conducted by a competent person on relevant parts of slaughtered or killed animals for the purpose of judging safety, suitability and disposition.
Defines “Primary production” as all steps in the food chain constituting animal production and transport of animals to the abattoir, or hunting and transporting wild game to a game depot.
Defines “Process control” as all conditions and measures applied during the production process that are necessary to achieve safety and suitability of meat.
Defines “Process criterion” as the physical process control parameters (e.g. time, temperature) at a specified step that can be applied to achieve a performance objective or performance criterion.
Defines the Quality Assurance (QA) system as the organizational structure, procedures, processes and resources needed to implement quality assurance.
Heading only for the primary production section; no substantive requirements or guidance provided in this text.
